author: Firstname Lastname
project_description: This is a repository for handling the data for '{{cookiecutter.project_name}}'.

__prompts__.author: Your full name
__prompts__.author_github_handle: Your GitHub username (used for links in generated docs)
__prompts__.project_slug: Python package name — auto-computed from project_name (no dashes)

publish_to_pypi.y: y — add publish step triggered on git tag

deptry.__prompt__: Add deptry for unused/missing dependency checking?

codecov.y: y — add codecov.yaml and upload step

devcontainer.__prompt__: Add VS Code Dev Container configuration?

type_checker.mypy: mypy — the classic, more configurable option

open_source_license.__prompt__: Open source license (choose 'Not open source' for proprietary/internal projects)
open_source_license.GNU General Public License v3: GPL v3 — copyleft, requires derivative works to be open

databricks_asset_bundle.__prompt__: Add Databricks Asset Bundle (DAB) support? Adds databricks.yml, notebooks/, resources/, and Azure DevOps DAB pipelines
databricks_asset_bundle.n: n — plain Python project, no Databricks files

__prompts__.databricks_catalog_prefix: Unity Catalog prefix — builds catalog names: {prefix}_dev, {prefix}_test, {prefix}_prod. Must be lowercase letters, digits and underscores (e.g. 'mdp', 'nrx', 'my_org')
__prompts__.databricks_catalog_suffix:
Unity Catalog suffix — appended after the environment to form the full catalog name.
  Leave EMPTY ('') for the recommended long-term pattern: {prefix}_{env} → mdp_dev / mdp_test / mdp_prod (one catalog per environment).
  Set to a layer name (e.g. 'bronze', 'silver', 'gold') to match the current infrastructure layout: {prefix}_{env}_{suffix} → mdp_dev_bronze / mdp_test_bronze / mdp_prod_bronze.
  NOTE: Per-layer catalogs are a transitional pattern. The long-term goal is per-environment catalogs (empty suffix) for better data isolation.
  Can also be set to a project-specific name for datasets with strict data-sensitivity requirements that need their own Azure Storage account.
__prompts__.databricks_schema_prefix: Schema prefix for this project — builds schema names: {prefix}_bronze, {prefix}_silver, {prefix}_gold. Encodes the domain/system/department, e.g. 'crm_dyn365', 'npb_volunteering', 'ipb_pims'

databricks_workspace_role.__prompt__: Workspace role suffix in DAB target names (e.g. 'in' → release_in_dev, 'ip' → release_ip_dev). Use 'in' (generic), 'ip' (Ingestion Process), or 'da' (Data Applications)

__prompts__.databricks_default_spark_version: Databricks Runtime (DBR) version for all job clusters (e.g. '13.3.x-scala2.12', '15.4.x-scala2.12'). Can be changed later in databricks.yml
__prompts__.databricks_workspace_host_dev: Databricks workspace URL for DEV environment (e.g. https://adb-1234567890.12.azuredatabricks.net). Safe to commit — not a secret.
__prompts__.databricks_workspace_host_test: Databricks workspace URL for TEST environment.
__prompts__.databricks_workspace_host_prod: Databricks workspace URL for PROD environment.
__prompts__.ado_service_connection_prefix: Azure DevOps service connection name prefix. Produces 'SC-dev', 'SC-test', 'SC-prod' (pattern: {prefix}-{env}). Must have Get+List on the Key Vaults.
__prompts__.ado_keyvault_name_dev: Azure Key Vault name for DEV credentials (contains databricks-sp-client-id, databricks-sp-secret).
__prompts__.ado_keyvault_name_test: Azure Key Vault name for TEST credentials.
__prompts__.ado_keyvault_name_prod: Azure Key Vault name for PROD credentials.
__prompts__.ado_agent_pool_dev: Azure Pipelines agent pool for DEV stage.
__prompts__.ado_agent_pool_test: Azure Pipelines agent pool for TEST stage.
__prompts__.ado_agent_pool_prod: Azure Pipelines agent pool for PROD stage.
__prompts__.ado_environment_dev: Azure DevOps Environment name for DEV (used for deployment tracking and approval gates).
__prompts__.ado_environment_test: Azure DevOps Environment name for TEST.
__prompts__.ado_environment_prod: Azure DevOps Environment name for PROD (configure an approval check here for the manual gate).

data_contracts.__prompt__: Add data contract YAML files? (only relevant when databricks_asset_bundle=y) Adds data-contracts/ with field definitions and quality rules
data_contracts.y: y — add data-contracts/ folder with examples
